purpose: Inventory older closed ATP-N proof claims against the current implementation so stale or overbroad claims cannot satisfy ATP-NR13 release proof aggregation.

policy.history_policy: Closed Beads are not deleted or rewritten; stale proof claims are made explicit and routed to live ATP-NR gates.
policy.release_policy: No historical ATP-N closure in this inventory directly satisfies ATP-NR13. ATP-NR13 must consume current ATP-NR gates and fresh artifacts.
policy.stale_claim_release_policy: Entries marked stale_overbroad, superseded_by_atp_nr, or blocked_frontier are release-blocking unless their replacement gates are green.
policy.foundation_policy: Entries marked accepted_foundation may remain valid foundational evidence but still require current ATP-NR release gates before signoff.

release_proof_inputs.blocked_release_reason: Historical ATP-N proof closures contain broad claims that are not equivalent to the current ATP-NR release proof aggregate.

status_catalog.meaning: The closed claim has live, focused artifacts that remain useful as foundation evidence, but it is not a complete release proof by itself.
The older closure is too broad for release signoff and must be replaced by the named ATP-NR gate or gates.
The older closure conflicts with live implementation or coverage reality and cannot be cited as current proof.
The replacement proof is known but currently blocked by implementation, environment, or shared-main frontier work.

entries.title: ATP-N1: Define per-module unit/property/metamorphic test contract and coverage ledger
entries.claimed_guarantee: Every ATP module has a per-module unit, property, and metamorphic test contract plus a coverage ledger that release gates can reference.
entries.current_command_result: Not rerun as a release proof. Live docs/atp_coverage_ledger.md reports TESTED 0, PARTIAL 0, PLANNED 33 and lists all critical path modules as PLANNED.

live_evidence.finding: The compliance summary says TESTED 0 and PLANNED 33; critical path modules remain release blockers.
The contract requires coverage, property, cancellation, leak, and performance gates that the ledger does not mark complete.
The test validates that ledger rows and required sections exist; it does not prove all module tests are implemented.

reconciliation_notes: Keep N1 as documentation foundation only.
Use ATP-NR3 for current per-module coverage proof.

entries.title: ATP-N2: Build native QUIC protocol conformance, fuzz, and deterministic packet e2e harness
entries.claimed_guarantee: Native QUIC has conformance, fuzz, deterministic packet-lab, and local two-endpoint e2e proof evidence.
entries.current_command_result: Not rerun as release proof. Live ATP-NR8 remains the current gate for native QUIC endpoint e2e without external QUIC crates.

live_evidence.finding: QUIC conformance harness surface exists.
Fuzz harness surface exists.
Current endpoint contract is the live ATP-NR8 proof surface, not the old closure text.
Dependency gate artifact supports no external QUIC stack policy.

reconciliation_notes: The old N2 closure remains useful inventory, but ATP-NR8 is the current release proof gate.
Do not cite N2 alone for complete native QUIC endpoint proof.

entries.title: ATP-N3: Build object graph, manifest, disk, journal, verifier, and crash-resume e2e proof suite
entries.close_reason: ATP-N3 object graph, journal, verifier crash-resume e2e proof suite implemented
entries.claimed_guarantee: Object graph, manifest, disk, journal, verifier, and crash-resume e2e proof suite exists for receiver safety.
entries.current_command_result: Not rerun as release proof. The current ledger still marks object, manifest, verifier, proof, journal, and writer modules PLANNED.

live_evidence.finding: Object and journal e2e proof-suite surface exists.
File-object e2e slice exists.
Journal append/recovery e2e slice exists.
Live module coverage status contradicts a full object/journal/verifier release-proof claim.

reconciliation_notes: Treat N3 as partial object/journal foundation.
Use ATP-NR6 and ATP-NR7 for current object-transfer and crash/resume release evidence.

entries.title: ATP-N5: Build CLI, atpd, SDK, and user-journey e2e scripts with structured log bundles
entries.close_reason: Added ATP CLI user-journey contract module, structured JSONL bundle runner, and tests/cli validator; script covers CLI/atpd/SDK journeys, receive safety, path fallback, proof/replay artifact discovery.
entries.claimed_guarantee: CLI, atpd, SDK, and real user-journey e2e scripts exist with structured log bundles.
entries.current_command_result: Not rerun as release proof. CLI/SDK/daemon journeys now route through ATP-NR5, ATP-NR10, and ATP-NR13.

live_evidence.finding: CLI user-journey validator exists.
atpd app-spec contract surface exists.
Current workflow perf/usability acceptance contract exists.
Structured log schema now comes from ATP-N6 rather than this older closure.

reconciliation_notes: The old user-journey contract is a useful foundation.
It does not replace the current no-mock multi-process and CLI/SDK/daemon NR gates.

entries.title: ATP-N6: Define structured logging, trace redaction, failure bundle, and replay artifact contract for all ATP tests
entries.close_reason: Implemented ATP-N6 structured log, failure-bundle, redaction, and replay-artifact schema contract.
entries.claimed_guarantee: ATP tests have a structured logging, trace redaction, failure-bundle, and replay-artifact schema contract.
entries.claimed_proof_command: RCH_REQUIRE_REMOTE=1 rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_p9 cargo test -p asupersync --test atp_e2e_proof_suite test_forensics_uses_logging_failure_bundle_replay_contract -- --nocapture
entries.current_command_result: Accepted as current foundation evidence. ATP-NR4 golden-log corpus also exercises the structured logging contract.

live_evidence.finding: Canonical log schema documentation exists.
Structured log schema implementation exists.
Failure-bundle implementation exists.
Deterministic golden-log corpus validates the structured logging contract.

reconciliation_notes: This is a legitimate completed foundation.
It still does not prove every ATP e2e, platform, and release aggregation lane.

entries.title: ATP-N7: Build cross-platform CI and release proof lane matrix for ATP tests
entries.close_reason: Cross-platform CI and proof lane matrix implemented and landed
entries.claimed_guarantee: Cross-platform CI and named release proof lanes cover ATP tests and release signoff.
entries.current_command_result: Not rerun as release proof. The canonical proof status snapshot is dated 2026-05-08 and the ATP-NR11 cross-platform gate remains the current platform capability proof.

live_evidence.finding: Dependency lanes are documented, but this is not a fresh cross-platform execution result.
Canonical lane manifest exists and names proof commands.
Snapshot exists but is dated 2026-05-08 and is stale under ATP-NR0 policy.
Contract explicitly distinguishes green, scoped, frontier, and externally blocked proof rows.

reconciliation_notes: The proof-lane manifest remains useful governance.
Fresh cross-platform capability evidence must come from ATP-NR11 and ATP-NR13.

entries.title: ATP-N10: Enforce per-implementation Definition of Done for unit tests, e2e scripts, logs, and proof artifacts
entries.close_reason: DoD enforcement infrastructure implemented and landed
entries.claimed_guarantee: ATP Definition of Done enforcement infrastructure rejects inadequate evidence such as compile-only, no-log, no-replay, or external-runtime-smuggled closures.
entries.current_command_result: Accepted as governance foundation only. ATP-NR0 and ATP-NR13 remain the live truth table and release aggregation gates.

live_evidence.finding: Manual DoD checklist exists.
DoD validator script exists.
Automated DoD validation test surface exists.
ATP-NR0 dashboard contract is now the live completion truth table.

reconciliation_notes: It is not a substitute for the current ATP-NR dashboard and release proof aggregation.
